items.title: FAA Orders Grounding of MD-11 Planes After Kentucky Crash
items.description: UPS and FedEx had earlier removed cargo planes from service as the death toll in Louisville climbs to 14.

items.title: UPS Grounds MD-11 Fleet After Kentucky Cargo Jet Crash. What to Know.
items.description: United Parcel Service has decided to ground its MD-11 fleet “out of an abundance of caution and in the interest of safety” following the tragic crash of one of its cargo jets, the company said Friday.  At the end of 2024, UPS owned and operated 294 aircraft, including 38 MD-11 jets.  “Contingency plans are in place to ensure we can continue to deliver the reliable service our customers around the world count on,” the statement said.

items.title: UPS and FedEx Ground MD-11 Fleets After Deadly Kentucky Plane Crash
items.description: Boeing requested the move out of “an abundance of caution” as the death toll in Louisville climbed to 14.

items.title: Hegseth Warns US Defense Contractors: Speed Up Or Fade Away
items.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth is pushing contractors to speed up weapons production and pledged U.S. support to allies while overhauling Pentagon procurement.

items.title: Boeing: A Stock for the Long Haul
items.description: Readers weigh in on Boeing, Elon Musk, Bank of America, the 60/40 portfolio, and the Magnificent Seven.
